label: Make a payment
description: Enable users to pay for a government service securely and with confidence, with full handling of success, pending, and failure states.
use_when: A service requires payment before completion or progression.
Fees, fines, or charges are due to a government entity.
Payment may be made online, in person, or on behalf of someone else.
do_not_use_when: Eligibility or fee calculation logic only — no actual transaction.

building_blocks.notes: Used to send payment confirmation and receipts.
Required if payment must be linked to a verified user identity.

inputs.label: Fee amount
inputs.description: Calculated fee amount passed from the service context.

steps.label: Understand the payment requirement
steps.description: Explain what the payment is for, the amount, any deadline, and who can pay.

steps.label: Understand options and reductions
steps.description: If applicable: exemptions, instalments, pay now vs later, refund conditions.

steps.label: Review payment details
steps.description: User reviews amount, currency, recipient, and selected method before authorising.

steps.label: Authorise payment
steps.description: User authorises the payment via the selected method. Service hands off to payment provider.

steps.label: Assisted payment authorisation
steps.description: Agent or officer processes payment on behalf of the user.

steps.label: Payment outcome
steps.description: Display result: success, pending, or failed. Show reference number and next steps.

steps.label: Send confirmation notification
steps.description: Send receipt or status update via SMS, email, or in-app notification.

ux_considerations.text: Users must be able to trust and verify that a payment request is legitimate. Show issuing authority clearly.
Always offer at least one non-digital payment route (in-person, kiosk, or assisted).
Support save-and-return where the user may need to leave and come back before completing payment.

technical_notes.text: Payment operations must be idempotent — retrying must not result in double-charging.
All payment events (initiation, authorisation, outcome) must be logged with user reference and timestamp.
Pending status must trigger an async notification when the status resolves.
